incident: Password-only backup encryption: Backup ciphertext uses password-derived key (Argon2/scrypt/PBKDF2) with no threshold shares or hardware key
label: H1_positive

incident: Password-only backup encryption: KDF parameters below recommended minimums (Argon2id: <64MB memory, <3 iterations; scrypt: <N=2^20)
label: H1_positive

incident: Password-only backup encryption: Backup encryption has no hardware key component (e.g., YubiKey HMAC challenge-response)
label: H1_positive

incident: Password-only backup encryption: User followed recommendation -- Split backup key into 2-of-3 Shamir shares: password-derived share + guardian share + hardware key share
label: H1_clean

incident: Stale guardians: Guardian address has no on-chain activity (transactions sent) in the last 6 months
label: H2_positive

incident: Stale guardians: Guardian has not responded to a liveness check (on-chain heartbeat or signed attestation) within the configured period
label: H2_positive

incident: Stale guardians: Number of active guardians minus stale guardians is below or equal to the recovery threshold
label: H2_positive

incident: Stale guardians: User followed recommendation -- Send liveness check request to all guardians. Replace any who do not respond within 30 days.
label: H2_clean

incident: Quantum-vulnerable backup encryption: Backup ciphertext header contains an ephemeral secp256k1 public key indicating ECDH key exchange
label: H3_positive

incident: Quantum-vulnerable backup encryption: Backup stored via contract known to use ECDH-only encryption (e.g., Tornado Cash encrypted notes)
label: H3_positive

incident: Quantum-vulnerable backup encryption: Backup encryption metadata does not indicate symmetric-only encryption (AES-256 with password KDF)
label: H3_positive

incident: Quantum-vulnerable backup encryption: User followed recommendation -- Encrypt backup with AES-256-GCM using password-derived key (Argon2id). AES-256 is PQ-safe; the password is the anchor, not ECDH.
label: H3_clean

incident: No coercion resistance: Backup ciphertext has exactly one valid decryption key -- no hidden volume or deniable layer
label: H4_positive

incident: No coercion resistance: Any valid password deterministically produces the same seed -- attacker can verify they have the real one
label: H4_positive

incident: No coercion resistance: Wrong passwords produce decryption errors instead of plausible fake seeds (no Honey Encryption)
label: H4_positive

incident: No coercion resistance: User followed recommendation -- Implement False-Bottom Encryption (Ahmad et al. 2023): same ciphertext, two passwords, two plaintexts. Real seed under real password, decoy seed under coercion password.
label: H4_clean

incident: Non-deterministic secrets not backed up: Transaction interacts with a protocol known to generate non-deterministic per-action secrets (e.g., Tornado Cash note = random nullifier + random secret)
label: H5_positive

incident: Non-deterministic secrets not backed up: Non-deterministic secret was generated but no corresponding encrypted backup transaction was submitted on-chain
label: H5_positive

incident: Non-deterministic secrets not backed up: Wallet claims seed-only recovery but uses protocols with non-deterministic state
label: H5_positive

incident: Non-deterministic secrets not backed up: User followed recommendation -- Immediately create an encrypted on-chain or off-chain backup of the non-deterministic secret after each protocol interaction
label: H5_clean
